sources.claim_text: In the OpenAI Agents SDK, delegation is implemented through handoff objects: an agent can transfer control to another agent along with a description of when and why.
sources.quote_or_evidence_span: OpenAI Agents SDK documentation on Agent handoffs, tool definitions, and Runner execution model.

sources.claim_text: Well-delegated tasks require fewer revision cycles before downstream agents can consume their output.
sources.quote_or_evidence_span: Anthropic research blog on multi-agent architecture reliability and task routing patterns.

sources.claim_text: In LangGraph, delegation appears as conditional graph edges where a supervisor evaluates state and routes to the appropriate sub-agent.
sources.quote_or_evidence_span: LangGraph documentation on supervisor agent patterns and conditional routing.

sources.claim_text: AutoGen's GroupChat model delegates by speaker selection, but the lack of structured task objects means delegation is implicit in conversation turn order.
sources.quote_or_evidence_span: Microsoft AutoGen documentation on GroupChat, speaker selection strategies, and termination conditions.

sources.claim_text: In CrewAI, delegation is explicit: Task objects carry a description, an expected output, an agent assignment, and a tool list.
sources.quote_or_evidence_span: CrewAI documentation on Task objects, expected_output fields, and agent role assignment.

sources.claim_text: Beyond depth 3, the final agent's understanding of the original task is substantially distorted unless each intermediate delegation restates the core objective.
sources.quote_or_evidence_span: Aggregated practitioner reports from technical blogs on multi-agent pipeline failures.

sources.claim_text: An agent told to 'find competitor pricing' with access to web-scraping, email-sending, and database-write tools may use any or all of them without explicit tool constraints.
sources.quote_or_evidence_span: Practitioner reports on authority boundary failures in tool-equipped agent systems.
